---
title: "2.1 Algorithms with Selection and Repetition"
subtitle: "Selection and Iteration"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Control Structures

### Algorithms are more than straight-line code

Until now, most beginner programs have followed plain sequential execution.  
This unit adds two new control moves:

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="term">selection</span>: choose between different paths
- <span class="term">repetition</span>: repeat steps until a condition changes
:::

![Sequencing, selection, and repetition](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-1-algorithms-with-selection-and-repetition/algorithms.png){fig-align="center" width="46%"}

## Selection

### Decision making based on true or false

Selection happens when an algorithm checks a condition and then follows a branch.

::: {.tight-list}
- “if there is a text, answer it”
- “if it is cold, wear a sweater”
- “if homework is due, pack it”
:::

The key idea is that <span class="mark">the next step depends on a Boolean result</span>.

## Repetition

### Doing something again until the work is finished

Repetition happens when an algorithm loops.

::: {.tight-list}
- keep snoozing for 15 minutes
- answer all of the texts
- repeat packing until the bag is ready
:::

In code, repetition becomes <span class="term">iteration</span> through loops such as `while` and `for`.

## Pseudocode and Flowcharts

### Plan the logic before writing Java

::: {.two-col}
::: {}
![Control-flow branching](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-1-algorithms-with-selection-and-repetition/Condition-two.png){width="100%"}
:::
::: {.soft-box}
Pseudocode and flowcharts help students:

- see algorithm order clearly
- mark branch points
- identify repeated work
- test the logic before Java syntax
:::
:::

The classroom move here is to separate <span class="term">algorithm design</span> from language syntax.

## Ordering Matters

### Same building blocks, different outcomes

An algorithm's result depends on:

::: {.tight-list}
- the order of steps
- which condition is checked first
- whether repetition stops at the right time
:::

That is why tracing pseudocode is part of the teaching sequence, not optional cleanup.

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- pseudocode and flowchart ordering checks
- identifying selection phrases and repetition phrases
- tracing gift-buying algorithm outcomes
- <span class="term">2.1.4 Coding Challenge: Algorithms</span>
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- define <span class="term">sequencing</span>, <span class="term">selection</span>, and <span class="term">repetition</span>
- explain that selection depends on a true/false decision
- explain that repetition continues until a stopping condition is met
- recognize pseudocode and flowcharts as planning tools
- explain why order affects algorithm outcome
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
